post.title: Did You Know Your Breathing Limits Your Voice? 👀
post.body: ⭐Nov '25  • Phase 1 - Pitch Training Q&ADid You Know Your Breathing Limits Your Voice? 👀You won't sing better if you don't breath better.Ever feel out of breath before, during, or after singing?You might be breathing improperly.Your oxygen is a fuel for your voice. It is the life force to singing. It helps you bring your sound to your voice.If you're doing this on your own, one thing to remember is, you have to be completely self-aware with how you speak and how you breathe and how you sing.Everything is connected.BUT, find balance. Too much of anything is not the best for you.On M... See moreYes, I learned to control it well. (Share what helps you below!)No, I need to learn how. (Share what your struggle is below!)129 votes

post.author: Emma Scott
post.title: Let’s Turn Your Ideas Into Amazing Songs 🎶
post.body: 16d  • I Need Feedback 🎥Let’s Turn Your Ideas Into Amazing Songs 🎶Hi everyone, I’m Emma Scott! 👋I’m a songwriter and music producer who loves helping artists bring their ideas to life. Whether you’re working on lyrics, melodies, or a full track, I’m here to support you with honest feedback and creative input.If you’ve got a song in progress or even just a small idea, feel free to share it I’d love to listen and help you make it even better. Let’s grow together and create something amazing! 🎧✨

I truly believe music grows best when we support and inspire each other, so I’m excited to be part of this space and connect with talented artists like yourself.Looking forward to sharing ideas, giving feedback, and seeing all the amazing music everyone creates here 🎶✨

post.title: Did You Know Your Breathing Limits Your Voice? 👀
post.body: ⭐Nov '25  • Phase 1 - Pitch Training Q&ADid You Know Your Breathing Limits Your Voice? 👀You won't sing better if you don't breath better.Ever feel out of breath before, during, or after singing?You might be breathing improperly.Your oxygen is a fuel for your voice. It is the life force to singing. It helps you bring your sound to your voice.If you're doing this on your own, one thing to remember is, you have to be completely self-aware with how you speak and how you breathe and how you sing.Everything is connected.BUT, find balance. Too much of anything is not the best for you.On M... See moreYes, I learned to control it well. (Share what helps you below!)No, I need to learn how. (Share what your struggle is below!)129 votes

post.author: Zane Rima
post.title: Did You Know Your Breathing Limits Your Voice? 👀
post.body: ⭐Nov '25  • Phase 1 - Pitch Training Q&ADid You Know Your Breathing Limits Your Voice? 👀You won't sing better if you don't breath better.Ever feel out of breath before, during, or after singing?You might be breathing improperly.Your oxygen is a fuel for your voice. It is the life force to singing. It helps you bring your sound to your voice.If you're doing this on your own, one thing to remember is, you have to be completely self-aware with how you speak and how you breathe and how you sing.Everything is connected.BUT, find balance. Too much of anything is not the best for you.On M... See moreYes, I learned to control it well. (Share what helps you below!)No, I need to learn how. (Share what your struggle is below!)129 votes

post.author: Zane Rima
post.title: Did You Know Your Breathing Limits Your Voice? 👀
post.body: ⭐Nov '25  • Phase 1 - Pitch Training Q&ADid You Know Your Breathing Limits Your Voice? 👀You won't sing better if you don't breath better.Ever feel out of breath before, during, or after singing?You might be breathing improperly.Your oxygen is a fuel for your voice. It is the life force to singing. It helps you bring your sound to your voice.If you're doing this on your own, one thing to remember is, you have to be completely self-aware with how you speak and how you breathe and how you sing.Everything is connected.BUT, find balance. Too much of anything is not the best for you.On M... See moreYes, I learned to control it well. (Share what helps you below!)No, I need to learn how. (Share what your struggle is below!)129 votes

generation.reply: There is not a singer in the world that doesn't struggle with this. . . Even grammy-winning singers like JohnnySwim. Their breath capacity is gigantic. And still, in a live video, Abbie takes a huge gasp breath, panics, and almost misses a note. This is the highest level of singing. So how do they avoid messing up? They plan where to take a breath far in advance. . . Like 3 days before the performance, they decide we will breathe HERE, HERE, and HERE. And then with the band's music sheet, they draw lines: inhale, inhale, inhale. All the singers do it. They write INHALE right on the music timeline.
